summary: Implemented the side-effect-free Opportunity Analysis Agent and addressed the second QA REQUEST_CHANGES by refining negated policy cue handling so no-code product descriptors do not suppress output-only unsafe or regulated topic classification.

tests_run.result: PASS: 32 tests passed, including regression coverage for output-only gambling/legal drift and output-only no-code gambling/legal drift.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. Nonfatal local SWC native-package warnings were printed before fallback build continued.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed line-ending conversion warnings for README.md, agents/index.ts, and several pre-existing project/*.json files.
PASS: handoff JSON parses and status is REVIEW.

decisions: Kept the module under agents/ and exported a stable analyzeOpportunity() entry point plus schemas and prompt/fallback helpers.
Used only injected LlmClient plus existing safeJsonCompletion(); no process.env reads, provider wiring, network calls, database writes, scoring engine, orchestrator, API, or UI work.
Made the heuristic fallback deterministic from Radar Task, keyword candidate, SERP analysis, and SERP results.
Applied risk guardrails after both LLM and fallback output so malformed claims are sanitized or escalated even when LLM JSON validates.
QA fix: excluded-topic and regulated-topic classification now scans LLM output fields in addition to original input, while ignoring clearly negated safety-policy chunks such as do-not/avoid language.
Second QA fix: hasNegatedPolicyCue() now treats no-code/no code as product descriptors rather than policy negations, while still ignoring explicit safety phrases such as do not build gambling tools.
Legal, tax, medical, and financial topics are constrained to checklist/self-assessment framing or excluded when above the radar task max risk.
Adult, gambling, gray-market, harmful, and configured excluded topics are marked excluded with monetization disabled.
risks: The heuristic fallback is intentionally conservative and may need domain tuning after real scan data is available.
Claim sanitization is regex-based guardrail logic; reviewers should inspect product-language edge cases before broad LLM use.
The successful build still emitted nonfatal local Next SWC native-package warnings about next-swc.win32-x64-msvc being an invalid Win32 application.
requested_action: Review the QA-requested no-code negation fix and route to required systems_architect/domain_designer review for acceptance.
